description:
You are a civil engineer analyzing a potential residential development site near Colorado Springs. Raw terrain survey data (site_survey.csv, 300 points, UTM Zone 13N) and project specifications (ProjectSpec.txt) are in C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\GradingStudy\.

Using TopoCal, build a terrain model from the survey data and determine the optimal grading elevation where earthwork is balanced — meaning cut volume approximately equals fill volume (cut-to-fill ratio between 0.9 and 1.1). Systematically test at least 5 candidate elevations, narrowing toward the balance point.

Produce three deliverables in the GradingStudy folder:
- grading_report.txt: each elevation tested with its cut and fill volumes (m³), and the final balanced elevation with its ratio
- site_grading.dxf: terrain contour map at 2-meter intervals
- site_grading.top: saved TopoCal project

metadata.location: Colorado Springs, El Paso County, Colorado
metadata.anti_gaming_note: Max partial credit without full marks on any criterion = 6+15+15+10+6 = 52, below pass threshold 60
